title: Writing: Describing Objects and People
learningGoal: Construct descriptive sentences ensuring that the adjective matches the gender of the noun, and the verb matches the pronoun.
instructions: In this writing task, you must pay extreme attention to grammar. You will describe a car (feminine), a house (masculine), and talk to a friend (informal). Choose the correct verb (hai/hain/ho/hoon) and the correct adjective ending (a/e/i).
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi:
1. The house is big.
2. The car is good. ('Car' is feminine)
3. These are books.
4. You are my friend. (Use informal 'Tum')
promptGoal: Applying adjective gender agreement and pronoun-verb conjugation.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: घर बड़ा है। गाड़ी अच्छी है। ये किताबें हैं। तुम मेरे दोस्त हो।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Ghar bada hai. Gaadi achchhi hai. Ye kitaabein hain. Tum mere dost ho.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: The house is big. The car is good. These are books. You are my friend.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Flawless grammar. 'Gaadi' takes 'achchhi'. 'Tum' takes 'ho'. Plural 'Ye' takes plural 'hain'.

evaluationCriteria.description: Uses 'bada' for ghar and 'achchhi' for gaadi.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: घर बड़ा है। गाड़ी अच्छी है। ये किताबें हैं। तुम मेरे दोस्त हो।
acceptableAlternates: Ghar bada hai. Gaadi achchhi hai. Ye kitaabein hain. Tum mere dost ho.
ghar bada hai. gaadi achchhi hai. ye kitaabein hain. tum mere dost ho.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict checking of adjective endings and verb matches.

feedbackRules.onPass: Masterful! You can now write grammatically sound descriptive sentences in Hindi.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the verb conjugation chart. Remember: Main hoon, Aap hain, Tum ho, Yeh hai.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Check your adjective endings. If the noun is feminine (like Gaadi), the adjective MUST end in 'i' (Achchhi).

hintText: Read the sentences out loud to check if they sound right. 'Tum... hai' should sound wrong to your ear now.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Gaadi achchha hai'. (Failing to make the adjective feminine).
